one_line: Maritime emperor of the Cholas who carried the waters of the Ganga and led naval campaigns toward Kadaram.

appearance.age_look: early 40s, tall and athletic with a commanding, battle-tested build
appearance.face: oval face with strong jaw, medium-brown South Indian complexion, sharp nose, thick brows, neatly trimmed moustache with short chin stubble, default expression calm but intense with slightly narrowed eyes conveying strategic focus
appearance.hair: thick black hair pulled back into a high, smooth topknot at the crown, wrapped with a narrow gold cloth band; no crown, but a simple gold forehead chain resting above the brows with a central small red gemstone
appearance.costume: royal Chola warrior-king attire: upper body mostly bare except for a deep-red silk angavastram draped from left shoulder across chest and back; fitted gold armlets on both biceps with simple leaf motifs; multiple short, heavy gold necklaces close to the collarbone and one longer necklace reaching mid-chest; forearms wrapped with plain cream cotton bracers; lower body in a finely pleated white cotton veshti/dhoti tied at the waist, front pleats falling to mid-calf with a slightly shorter rear fold, bordered by a 2-inch gold stripe; over the veshti, a broad gold-plated waistbelt with engraved geometric patterns, snug but not bulky; bare feet with simple leather sandals of dark brown straps; no fantastical armour, only subtle leather padding visible at shoulders under the angavastram, matching the era’s practical minimal protection
appearance.signature: a long Chola-style steel sword with a gently curved single-edged blade, plain bright-polished finish, and a distinctive hilt: dark wood grip with a simple disc pommel and small protective crosspiece, carried in a dark brown leather scabbard hanging from his left side on a plain strap

ref_desc: Rajendra Chola I is a tall, medium-brown warrior-king with a high black topknot, gold forehead chain, deep-red angavastram, white veshti with gold border, gold ornaments, leather sandals, and a curved Chola steel sword in a dark brown scabbard, using red, white, gold, brown, and black tones.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style character of Rajendra Chola I as a dignified Chola emperor: tall, athletic, medium-brown South Indian man in his early forties with an oval face, strong jaw, sharp nose, thick brows, and a neatly trimmed moustache with short chin stubble, wearing his black hair pulled into a high topknot wrapped with a narrow gold cloth band and a simple gold forehead chain with a small red stone. Show his upper body mostly bare, adorned with short, heavy gold necklaces, gold armlets on both biceps, and cream cotton bracers on his forearms, with a deep-red silk angavastram draped from his left shoulder across his chest and back. His lower body has a finely pleated white cotton veshti/dhoti with a clear gold border, tied at the waist under a broad engraved gold-plated waistbelt, and he stands in simple dark brown leather sandals, with only subtle shoulder padding implied beneath the cloth, not visible as armor. At his left side hangs his signature Chola sword in a dark brown leather scabbard: a long, gently curved single-edged steel blade with a plain polished finish and a dark wood hilt with disc pommel and small crosspiece. Use a clean, heroic anime look with deep red, white, gold, dark brown, and black as the dominant colors, front, side, and back views implied in one image by clear, unobstructed depiction of face, hair, clothes, and sword.
